pre_registration.target_metric: cites-based body-text integration rate 6 sessions post-flag
pre_registration.success_criterion: rate stable or increasing post-S429 structural flag
pre_registration.falsification: if gap grows and rate declines in first 6 sessions

expect: Cross-domain body-text integration rate will be measurable at <1%; structural enforcement can increase to >=2%
actual: Cites-based rate: 32.4% (S429) -> 32.0% (S435), -0.4pp. New lessons rate 24.1% vs 32.4% baseline (-8.3pp). Gap grew +22. Substantive discussion rate (L-964 definition) estimated still ~0.1% — tool measures weak mention, not synthesis.
diff: Expected flag adoption to be neutral-to-positive. Actual: negative adoption signal in new lessons. Auto-harvest Cites: mechanism creates citations faster than body integration follows. L-601 creation-time enforcement required, not post-facto flags.
verdict: EARLY-NEGATIVE (6/20 sessions): structural detection without creation-time enforcement shows zero adoption. Cites-based rate declining. Target measure at S449: if no enforcement change, expect gap 450+, rate <30%.
